issuesFound.fix: Line 695: `#search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter buttons remove the focus indicator. Keyboard users navigating the product filters will have no visual feedback on their current position.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1004: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion triggers remove the focus outline. This prevents keyboard users from identifying which question they are about to expand.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1354: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the focus outline. Without a visible replacement that works in all viewing modes, the form is inaccessible to keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1396: `.form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open, close, and toggle functions update CSS classes and scroll-lock states but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the hamburger trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation drawer has been deployed or dismissed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1575: open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }
Line 1599: close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing billing-period buttons (Monthly/Annual) are updated visually via classes, but their aria-pressed state is never modified. Users of assistive technology will not know which billing cycle is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2174: _periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const btnPeriod = btn.getAttribute('data-period'); const isActive = btnPeriod === period; btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(isActive)); toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); });

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function inserts error messages and updates aria-describedby, but it never sets the aria-invalid attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state when focus enters.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2715: function _renderResult(field, result, errorEl) { if (result.valid) { field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); ... } else { field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); ... } }

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator.clearField function resets the visual error state but does not remove or update the aria-invalid attribute. A field previously marked as invalid will continue to be announced as such even after being cleared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2755: function clearField(field) { ... removeClass(field, CSS.fieldValid); field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); }

issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" during the init function. Assistive technologies may not correctly identify the element as a toggleable menu until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1640: function init() { ... _initialised = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open, close, and toggle functions change the visible state of the drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav module, within the open function add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); within the close function add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (role="switch") does not update aria-checked when toggled. Screen reader users cannot perceive the current state (month/year) of the switch.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncToggleState function, add: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the result count or that content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: In the applyFilters function, after updating the count badge, add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found.`);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates all price displays but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users may not know that prices have been updated.
issuesFound.fix: In the setPeriod function, after updating the prices, add: liveRegion.announce(`Switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()} billing.`);

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked invalid, the validator does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the invalid state of the field.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult function, when result.valid is false, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); when result.valid is true, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialised on page load. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav init function, after obtaining _trigger, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialised with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state of dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: In the dropdownMenu register function, after setting aria-haspopup and aria-controls, add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing comparison table trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table expand/collapse trigger does not have aria-expanded initialised on page load. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the pricing init function, after obtaining the trigger via qs('[data-expand-comparison]'), if it exists, add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, aria-hidden is not set to "true". The drawer remains in the accessibility tree when hidden, causing potential confusion.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav close function, add: setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); In the open function, add: setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop click missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page but does not announce the action. Screen reader users may not know the page has scrolled to the top.
issuesFound.fix: In the _onClick function, after scrolling, add: liveRegion.announce('Scrolled to top of page.');

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips removal missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Removing a filter chip updates the UI but does not announce the change. Screen reader users may not know the filter has been removed and results updated.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1156: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1165: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function sets up event listeners for the navigation toggle but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` state. This leaves the initial state of the toggle ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1235: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visibility of the main navigation, but there is no corresponding write to a live region to announce "Navigation menu opened" or "closed".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1156: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`, Line 1165: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function removes the visible class from the drawer and overlay, but it does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1165: `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds the `CSS.fieldInvalid` class and updates the error message, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2568: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function handles the valid state by removing the error class, but it does not explicitly set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2563: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the default outline with “outline: none” and only sets outline-offset. No visible focus style (box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline) is provided, leaving keyboard users without a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance(inst)` and `_closeInstance(inst)` change the visibility of a dropdown panel but never set or update `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Search component does not update aria‑expanded on the input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_open()` and `_close()` helpers in the site‑search module toggle the suggestion list visibility but never set `aria‑expanded` on the search `<input>`, breaking the combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open()` call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close()` call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded = false
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the trigger button receives `aria-controls` but no initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its collapsed state is not conveyed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: In `register(trigger, panel)` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger missing initial aria‑expanded = false
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are parsed and given `aria-controls`, but the trigger button is never given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, leaving its collapsed state undisclosed.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the trigger in `faqRegistry.parse`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing initial aria‑expanded = false
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is given `role="combobox"` and `aria-controls`, but no initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its closed state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: In `siteSearch.init` after setting other ARIA attributes, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance(inst)` hides the dropdown panel by adding a CSS class and setting `hidden`, but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel, so the element remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding the panel, call `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ answer panel hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `close(item)` sets `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`, leaving the hidden answer still perceivable by assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` when closing, and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `suggestionListRenderer.hide()` sets the `hidden` attribute on the listbox but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`, so the list remains in the accessibility tree when closed.
issuesFound.fix: In `hide()` add `setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `show()` remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge(count)` changes the numeric text of the product count badge, but no ARIA live region is used, so screen‑reader users are unaware of result‑count changes.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge, set `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`)` or add an `aria-live="polite"` element to receive the update.

issuesFound.title: FAQ open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item)` and `close(item)` functions emit custom events but never update a live region, so users are not told when an answer is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: After changing state, call `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer ${item.open ? 'expanded' : 'collapsed'}`)`.

issuesFound.title: Search component open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion list is shown or hidden, no live‑region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback that suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open()` call `liveRegion.announce('Search suggestions opened')`; in `_close()` call `liveRegion.announce('Search suggestions closed')`.

fpTitles: FAQ answer panel hidden without aria‑hidden
FAQ open/close actions are not announced via a live region
Search component open/close actions are not announced via a live region

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` in the dropdown menu controller add or remove the “open” class and hide/show the panel, but they never set or clear `aria-expanded` on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance` add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not set aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `close()` function of `mobileNav` adds the `hidden` attribute to the drawer but never sets `aria-hidden="true"`. When the drawer is hidden it remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After adding `hidden`, also call `drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) controls the drawer (`_drawer`) but no `aria-controls` attribute is set, so assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: After locating the drawer, add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-controls', drawer.id)` (ensure the drawer has an `id`).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function updates the displayed prices but never announces the change to screen‑reader users. Users may not realize the billing period has switched.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period switched to ' + period)`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period simple switch does not set aria‑pressed or aria‑checked
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off toggle (`_onToggleClick`) changes the period but the element has `role="switch"` without updating `aria-checked`/`aria-pressed`, leaving its state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, set `toggle.setAttribute('aria-checked', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` adds error messages and CSS classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field, so AT does not know the field is erroneous.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate`, when `!result.valid` call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on contact form “full_name” field
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 7, affecting the contact form’s “Full name” input.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 7.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on contact form “email” field
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 7, affecting the contact form’s email input.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 7.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on contact form “message” textarea
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 7, affecting the contact form’s message textarea.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 7.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed (`_closeInstance`) the code removes the “open” class and adds `hidden`, but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` to the panel, leaving it in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `hidden`, also call `panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`; when opening, remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panel hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `close(item)` function hides the answer panel with `hidden` but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`.
issuesFound.fix: Add `item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')` when closing; remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element receives `hidden` when closed but never receives `aria-hidden="true"`.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()`, after adding `hidden`, also set `drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()`, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles between monthly and yearly billing has `role="switch"` but does not update `aria-pressed`/`aria-checked`, making its state invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After each toggle, set `button.setAttribute('aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle does not reference the region it controls (the price display), so screen readers cannot associate them.
issuesFound.fix:
Assign an `id` to the price container and set `button.setAttribute('aria-controls', priceContainerId)`.

These issue blocks satisfy all required JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and highlight missing ARIA state updates, missing live‑region announcements, and missing ARIA attributes on toggle controls.

fpTitles: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on contact form “full_name” field
Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on contact form “email” field
Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on contact form “message” textarea

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function shows the panel but never sets aria‑expanded on the trigger button, so screen‑reader users cannot know the menu state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is expanded the trigger button’s aria‑expanded state is never changed, leaving assistive technology unaware of the panel’s visibility.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button changes the navigation drawer but never reflects its pressed state via aria‑pressed, which is required for toggle controls.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that open/close a dropdown act as binary toggles but never set aria‑pressed, so assistive technologies cannot convey their state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing‑period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period selector buttons toggle between “monthly”, “quarterly”, and “annual” but never expose their pressed state via aria‑pressed.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the number of visible products changes only the visual badge is updated; no live‑region announcement is made, leaving users of assistive technology unaware of the result.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change does not announce the new period
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly/quarterly/annual pricing updates the UI but provides no live‑region feedback, so screen‑reader users do not know which plan is now displayed.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: Validation results are reflected only by CSS classes and error messages; the required aria‑invalid state is never set, breaking the ARIA mapping of native required attributes.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panels are hidden without aria‑hidden**
issuesFound.explanation: Panels are concealed by the hidden attribute only; they remain in the accessibility tree, causing screen‑reader confusion.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer is hidden without aria‑hidden**
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer is hidden by CSS classes only; it stays perceivable to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot identify which element has focus. Box-shadow is not a valid replacement in High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative like a border or non-zero outline. This hides focus state from keyboard users interacting with buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an offset, resulting in no visible focus indicator. This affects keyboard navigation for the mobile menu toggle.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator replaced with box-shadow on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus rule removes the outline and relies solely on box-shadow. Box-shadows are invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode, blocking access for users relying on that mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the outline and rely on box-shadow for focus styling. This fails in High Contrast Mode where box-shadows are suppressed, making form fields unreachable for some users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot track focus within the footer navigation region.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the outline on focus with only an offset. This makes the active filter indistinguishable for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the outline on focus. Since the entire card is clickable via pseudo-element, the link focus state must be visible to indicate keyboard interaction.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a minimum height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for touch targets. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets width and height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons require larger touch areas for accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has a fixed height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size and barely above the 24px minimum. It may be difficult to tap on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width/height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Custom checkboxes are sized at 20px, below the 24px minimum. This makes them difficult to select for users with limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle min-height/min-width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button is 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size (WCAG 2.5.8). While above the 24px minimum, it degrades the experience for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width/height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28px, below the 44px recommended size. This makes it hard to tap accurately on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Content meant for screen readers only will be visible to sighted users, cluttering the interface.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses a transform transition to slide into view. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 573 is empty, so this motion is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses a 3D transform transition on hover. The corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 838 is empty, failing to suppress the animation for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can cause letters to collide or become illegible for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.9:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p text color (#aaaaaa) on white background (#ffffff) has a contrast ratio of 2.9:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text. Users with low vision may not read this text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground rgba(255,255,255,0.3) / background gradient
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label text uses white at 30% opacity on a colored gradient background. This results in extremely low contrast, making statistics unreadable for many users.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove text-decoration and rely only on color change on hover to indicate interactivity. Color is not a sufficient visual indicator for links (SC 1.4.1).

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .filter-tabs button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size. This makes selecting filters difficult on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .pricing-toggle button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size. This degrades the experience for touch users switching billing cycles.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually while keeping it accessible. It effectively makes the content visible or does nothing, breaking the screen-reader-only intent.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle button does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle CSS classes and scroll locking but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (.nav-toggle). Screen reader users are not informed when the navigation menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1723: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1745: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle visibility via CSS classes but do not toggle aria-hidden on the drawer element (#main-nav). When closed, the drawer content remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1723: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 1745: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions manage the panel visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed when a dropdown opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1918: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1932: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion module's open() function sets the panel to visible but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3415: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: Search input does not update aria-expanded when suggestions toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module's _open() and _close() functions manage the suggestion list visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the search input (#search-input). Screen reader users are not informed when the suggestion list is displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2230: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 2240: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1395: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Form valid state does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the error description but fails to set aria-invalid="false" on the field. The field may remain in an invalid state for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1406: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Sticky header not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The stickyHeader module's hide() and show() functions toggle CSS classes to visually hide the header but do not toggle aria-hidden. The hidden header content remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1936: _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
  Line 1941: _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Viewport reveal elements not managed in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The viewportReveal module's _reveal() function removes the data-reveal attribute and adds a visible class but does not manage aria-hidden. Elements hidden by CSS before reveal remain in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5185: el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Stat counter updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator module's _animate() function updates the textContent of stat numbers ([data-stat-number]) dynamically but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the updated count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5285: liveRegion.announce(`Stat updated to ${_format(cfg.target, cfg)}`);

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter module's _updateCountBadge() function updates the text content of the count badge ([data-product-count]) when filters change but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2463: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing savings message updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module's _updateSavingsMessage() function updates the text content of the savings message ([data-savings-message]) when the billing period changes but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the new savings information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2893: liveRegion.announce(_savingsMsg.textContent);

issuesFound.title: Plan comparison highlighter changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The planComparisonHighlighter module's highlightColumn() function changes the visual highlight state of pricing columns ([data-plan-col]) on hover/click but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the highlighted plan.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3060: liveRegion.announce(`${planId} plan highlighted`);

issuesFound.title: Skip navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element wrapping the skip links lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. When multiple nav landmarks exist (as they do here with main, mobile, and footer navs), each must have a unique accessible name to allow screen reader users to distinguish them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 365: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav id="main-nav"> element lacks an aria-label. Since there is also a mobile navigation and a footer navigation on the page, this landmark requires a unique name (e.g., "Main") to be distinguishable in landmark navigation menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation inner landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav className="mobile-nav__body"> inside the mobile panel lacks an aria-label. While the parent dialog has a label, this nested nav landmark should also be named to ensure consistent landmark structure if queried by assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 515: <nav aria-label="Mobile" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: Hero section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section className="hero-section"> lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. As a major page region, it should be identifiable as a landmark. It contains an h1, which should be referenced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 630: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: Stats section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section className="stats-bar"> lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. It contains an h2#stats-heading which should be used to label the section landmark for screen reader navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 705: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: Mobile submenu trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> triggering the sub-menu in MobileNav controls the visibility of the submenu (isExpanded state) but does not have aria-expanded. Assistive technology cannot convey the open/closed state of the accordion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 500: <button ... aria-expanded={isExpanded} aria-controls={sectionId} ...>

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons in FilterTabs toggle an active state (activeFilter) visually via class names but lack aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive which category is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 865: <button ... aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} ...>

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle Monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The Monthly billing button toggles state visually but lacks aria-pressed. Assistive technology cannot convey that this option is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 965: <button ... aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} ...>

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle Annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The Annual billing button toggles state visually but lacks aria-pressed. Assistive technology cannot convey that this option is currently selected.

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <article> in PlanCard lacks aria-labelledby. While it contains a heading (h3#headingId), the article element does not reference it, failing to provide an accessible name for the card region.

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan CTA link missing specific accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The CTA link in PlanCard relies on visible text (e.g., "Start Pro free trial") which does not explicitly include the plan name in a way that guarantees context if read out of sequence. WCAG 2.4.4 recommends unique link names.

issuesFound.title: Required field marker announces asterisk to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The <span> containing the asterisk (*) in FormGroup lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce "asterisk" or "star" which is noise; the required state is already conveyed via aria-required on the input.

issuesFound.title: Button component spinner missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy state

issuesFound.title: Required field marker announces asterisk to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk indicating a required field is rendered in a <span> without aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce "asterisk" or "star" literally, which is confusing noise. The visual marker should be hidden from assistive technology while the "required" state is conveyed programmatically.

issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and border-radius. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users with no focus indicator on any interactive element.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset. In forced-colors mode, box-shadow is suppressed, so outline is the only reliable focus indicator. Removing it leaves high-contrast users with no focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the navigation drawer visibility by adding CSS classes and managing focus, but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the expanded state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after setting `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2170: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after setting `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function discovers the trigger element but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on page load, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial closed state of the navigation drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside the init function after verifying _trigger exists.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown menu panel, the _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _openInstance after setting `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function closes the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to communicate the closed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2270: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _closeInstance after setting `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function ensures the panel is hidden on initialisation but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, so screen readers do not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2320: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside the register function after setting `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands a FAQ item panel and sets item.open = true, but unlike the close function, it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3750: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside the open function after setting `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open function displays the suggestion listbox but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the combobox input element, violating the ARIA combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside the _open function after setting `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._close function hides the suggestion listbox but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the combobox input, leaving the input in an expanded state for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3010: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside the _close function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function configures the input as a combobox with aria-controls but never initialises aria-expanded="false" for the closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside the init function after mounting the listbox.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function toggles visual active states on filter tab buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect which tab is currently pressed/selected, failing the toggle button pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` inside the forEach loop after calculating isActive.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid helper function adds an error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form field, so screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4080: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside the markInvalid function after addDescribedBy.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid helper removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" (or remove the attribute) on the field, leaving screen readers with stale invalid state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4090: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` inside the markValid function after clearing the describedby references.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle missing aria-checked update
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function manages a switch control (role="switch") by toggling CSS classes but never updates the aria-checked attribute to reflect the on/off state, violating the ARIA switch pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3330: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', isYear ? 'true' : 'false');` inside _syncToggleState after calculating isYear.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function dynamically updates price amounts and billing period labels when users toggle between monthly and annual billing, but does not announce these changes via the liveRegion, leaving screen reader users unaware of the price update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3350: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices switched to ' + period + ' billing');` inside setPeriod after updating the display.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change missing live region announcement

issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` inside open function. Line 2170: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` inside close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show and hide dropdown panels but do not announce the expanded or collapsed state via the liveRegion, preventing screen readers from confirming the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` inside _openInstance. Line 2270: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` inside _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and close functions expand and collapse FAQ answer panels but do not announce the state change via the liveRegion, leaving screen reader users unaware whether the answer is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3750: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title));` inside open function. Line 3770: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title));` inside close function.

issuesFound.title: contactForm inputs missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contactForm.init function queries personal data inputs (first name, last name, email, company) but does not ensure they have autocomplete attributes (e.g., given-name, family-name, email, organization), preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill and reducing accessibility for users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4250: Add `setAttr(qs('#contact-first-name', _formEl), 'autocomplete', 'given-name');`, `setAttr(qs('#contact-last-name', _formEl), 'autocomplete', 'family-name');`, `setAttr(qs('#contact-email', _formEl), 'autocomplete', 'email');`, and `setAttr(qs('#contact-company', _formEl), 'autocomplete', 'organization');` inside the init function after querying the elements.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus management on open

issuesFound.fix:
Line 1862 (in open function): After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`, add:

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle functions add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open or closed state. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the navigation is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2840: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_lockScroll();` in the open function. Line 2860: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_unlockScroll();` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by toggling the hidden attribute, but it never sets aria-expanded on the trigger button to indicate the state change. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2972: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the end of _openInstance. Line 2987: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of _closeInstance before the setTimeout.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion combobox opens and closes the suggestion listbox, but it does not toggle aria-expanded on the search input element to communicate the visibility state of the suggestions to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3182: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in the _open function. Line 3192: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the _close function.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) visually indicate the active state via CSS classes, but they do not set aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to screen reader users. This is required for toggle buttons that cycle between two states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3732: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the _periodBtns.forEach loop after toggleClass.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the _renderResult function calls srUtils.markInvalid which only adds aria-describedby, but it never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4255: In the else block (when !result.valid), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` before or after the markInvalid call.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the code calls srUtils.markValid which removes aria-describedby, but it does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. The field may remain marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4250: In the if (result.valid) block, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after the markValid call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the code declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes or activates a focus trap. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer and into the background content, violating WCAG focus containment requirements for modal dialogs.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button starts in the closed state, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" on page load. Screen readers will not know the initial state of the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2820: After setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are registered and given aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" on page load when the panels are closed. This violates the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2940: After `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that controls the suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" on page load when the suggestions are hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: After `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering products, the visible result count updates in the DOM, but this change is not announced to screen reader users via a live region. Users are not informed how many results match their filter selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products shown`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually and via textContent, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3720: After `_syncToggleState(period);` add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced via a live region. While aria-expanded is set on the trigger, the content change itself is not announced, leaving screen reader users unaware of the panel content becoming available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4330: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title));`. Line 4350: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title));`

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner not hidden from assistive technology

issuesFound.fix: Line ~211: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.fix: Line ~275: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message ID that is not rendered because FormGroup lacks the errorId prop.

issuesFound.fix: Line ~410: <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.fix: Line ~375: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation landmark lacks an accessible name, making it indistinguishable from other navigation regions.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav inner navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element inside the mobile dialog lacks an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~438: <nav aria-label="Mobile" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk is announced as "star" by screen readers instead of being hidden and allowing the visually hidden "(required)" text to convey the information.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~249: *

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The button does not communicate its loading state to assistive technology, causing users to be unaware that the action is in progress.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~207: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div used as interactive element without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop element is clickable but is implemented as a div without button role or keyboard support, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~435: <div role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close navigation menu" className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} />

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation dialog does not declare itself as modal, allowing screen reader users to navigate outside the dialog while it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~446: <div id={menuId} ref={panelRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={`${SITE.name} navigation`} className={['mobile-nav', isOpen ? 'mobile-nav--open' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} hidden={!isOpen}>

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The modal dialog component does not declare itself as modal, failing to restrict screen reader navigation to the dialog content while open.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~948: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button in the navigation controls a sub-menu but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen readers from announcing whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1030: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation hamburger toggle button does not communicate its expanded state to assistive technology, leaving screen reader users unaware when the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1160: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the mobile toggle button.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section triggers missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that expand and collapse sub-sections within the mobile navigation have aria-controls but no aria-expanded attribute to indicate the current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1110: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the section trigger button.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button lacks aria-expanded, so screen readers cannot announce whether the answer panel is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1410: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the FAQ trigger button.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle is an icon-only button that does not provide an accessible name, making it unidentifiable to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: Add aria-label={LABELS[theme]} to the IconButton.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in the mobile navigation panel is icon-only and lacks an aria-label, providing no accessible name for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1095: Add aria-label="Close navigation" to the IconButton.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel prev/next IconButtons missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next carousel controls are icon-only buttons without aria-label attributes, leaving screen reader users unaware of their purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to IconButton. Line 1390: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" to IconButton.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly and annual billing toggle buttons visually indicate selection but do not communicate the pressed state to assistive technology using aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1455: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} to the monthly button. Line 1465: Add aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} to the annual button.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons indicate active state visually but lack aria-pressed, so screen readers cannot convey which filter is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1330: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to each filter button.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button for the background video is a toggle control that uses aria-label but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the playing state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 945: Add aria-pressed={isPlaying} to the Button component.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page navigation link has a visual current indicator but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from identifying it as the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1050: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active page link in the mobile menu has a visual current state but no aria-current="page" to communicate the current page to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1125: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links navigation landmark lacks an accessible name, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 585: Change <nav> to <nav aria-label="Skip links">.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation element lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation regions such as the mobile menu and skip links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1025: Change <nav id="main-nav" className="desktop-nav"> to <nav id="main-nav" className="desktop-nav" aria-label="Desktop">.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav internal nav missing aria-label

issuesFound.fix: Line 1105: Change <nav className="mobile-nav__body"> to <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile">.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section is a major page region but lacks an accessible name via aria-labelledby, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 925: Change <section className="hero-section"> to <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar section lacks aria-labelledby to provide an accessible name for the landmark region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1005: Change <section className="stats-bar"> to <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card is an article element in a repeated grid but lacks aria-labelledby referencing its heading, making it difficult to identify in a list of regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: Change <article className="pricing-card__article"> to <article className="pricing-card__article" aria-labelledby={headingId}>.

issuesFound.title: Trust badges group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The group of trust badges lacks an accessible name to describe the purpose of the group to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 965: Change <div className="hero-section__trust"> to <div className="hero-section__trust" aria-label="Trust badges">.

issuesFound.title: Hero CTAs group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The group of call-to-action buttons in the hero section lacks an accessible name to identify the group purpose.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav footer CTAs missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The footer CTA button group in the mobile menu lacks an accessible name.

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader CTAs missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The header CTA button group lacks an accessible name to distinguish it from other button groups.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1175: Change <div className="site-header__ctas"> to <div className="site-header__ctas" aria-label="Account actions">.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated integration list for visual looping is not hidden from assistive technology, causing duplicate content to be announced by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 995: Change <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list"> to <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true"> for the second (cloned) list.

issuesFound.title: StarRating stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Individual star symbols are not hidden from assistive technology despite the container having an aria-label, potentially causing redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 610: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span elements containing the star characters (★ and ☆).

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative chevron icon indicating dropdown state is not hidden from assistive technology and may be announced as text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1035: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span with className="nav-link__chevron".

issuesFound.title: Button spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner visual element is not hidden from assistive technology, while a separate VisuallyHidden element provides the accessible label, potentially causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 685: Change <span className="btn-spinner" /> to <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />.

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid, so screen readers cannot announce the error state of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 755: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the input element.

issuesFound.title: Textarea missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid, preventing screen readers from announcing validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 765: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the textarea element.

issuesFound.title: Select missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid, so the error state is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 785: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the select element.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email broken aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message ID via aria-describedby when in error state, but the FormGroup component was not provided the errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1620: Pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" to the FormGroup component, or remove the aria-describedby from the TextInput.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm fields broken aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: Form fields reference error message IDs via aria-describedby, but FormGroup is not provided the errorId prop, resulting in broken references to non-existent elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1670: Pass errorId="contact-name-error" to FormGroup. Line 1680: Pass errorId="contact-email-error" to FormGroup. Line 1690: Pass errorId="contact-subject-error" to FormGroup. Line 1700: Pass errorId="contact-message-error" to FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div click handler without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop element has an onClick handler to close the menu but is a div without role="button", tabIndex, or keyboard handlers, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1085: Change <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} /> to <button className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} aria-label="Close navigation" /> or add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div click handler without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay has an onClick handler to close the dialog but is a div without role="button" or keyboard accessibility, preventing keyboard users from activating it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1810: Change <div className="modal-overlay" ... onClick={onClose}> to a button element or add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler for Enter and Space keys.

issuesFound.title: Required field marker not aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker for required fields is not hidden from assistive technology, potentially causing "star" to be announced in addition to the "required" text provided by VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 805: Change * to *.

issuesFound.title: Button missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: The button component has a loading state but does not communicate this to assistive technology via aria-busy, which would indicate that the element is being updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 675: Add aria-busy={isLoading} to the button element.
